summary: Result switching is exposed directly on each Nano Banana item, and the selected result is consistently used for preview, download, and promote-to-source actions. Storyboard title save behavior remains in the existing DB save flow instead of introducing a parallel flow.

The storyboard change narrows the frontend save branch so metadata-only edits use the existing PATCH path. Nano Banana keeps compatibility with single-result payloads while extending the Electron and local server response model to arrays.

The changes address reported workflow blockers: title edits no longer unexpectedly create new records, and multiple AI edit outputs can be reviewed without rerunning work. No unrelated feature expansion was added.

Syntax checks and the primary build passed. Remaining risk is that live Vertex responses with multiple image parts need real API confirmation, but the code preserves the previous single-image behavior.

The implementation matches the user's stated expectations: title changes are edits, not new DB records, and multiple edit outputs are visible and selectable. Original source files are not deleted by list removal behavior.
The numbered result selector is compact and fits the existing utility-style Nano Banana item controls. No new visual language or layout pattern was introduced.

The change does not expand credential exposure or delete original files. Multi-result history reads and writes remain under the existing Nano Banana history directory and preserve the local-only storage model.
The Cloudflare-facing storyboard change is a frontend request routing correction and does not require a schema change or migration. Build verification passed before commit.
